literal-translation: And a river went out from Eden to water the garden and from there it was divided and became to four heads.
Name of the first Pishon it the surrounding all land of the Havilah where there the gold.
And gold of the land that good there the bdellium and stone of the onyx.
And name of the river the second Gihon it the surrounding all land of Cush.
And name of the river the third Tigris it the going east of Assyria and the river the fourth it Euphrates.
And took Yahweh God the man and set him in garden of Eden to till it and to keep it.
And commanded Yahweh God upon the man saying from every tree of the garden eating you shall eat.
And from tree of the knowledge good and evil not you shall eat from it because in day of eating you from it dying you shall die.
And said Yahweh God not good being the man to alone of him I will make to him helper according-to opposite of him.

And formed Yahweh God from the ground every beast of the field and every fowl of the heavens and brought to the man to see what he would call to it and all which called to it the man soul living it name of it.
And called the man names to all the cattle and to fowl of the heavens and to every beast of the field and to man not was found helper according-to opposite of him.
And caused-fall Yahweh God deep-sleep upon the man and he slept and took one from ribs of him and closed flesh instead of it.
And built Yahweh God the rib which he took from the man to woman and brought her to the man.
And said the man this time bone from bones of me and flesh from flesh of me to this shall be called Woman because from man was taken this.
Therefore shall leave man father of him and mother of him and shall cling in woman of him and they shall be to flesh one.
And they were both of them naked the man and woman of him and not they were ashamed.
And the serpent was shrewd from every beast of the field which made Yahweh God and he said to the woman indeed because said God not you shall eat from every tree of the garden.
And said the woman to the serpent from fruit of tree of the garden we may eat.
And from fruit of the tree which in middle of the garden said God not you shall eat from it and not you shall touch in it lest you die.
And said the serpent to the woman not dying you shall die.
